channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Dodgers' Shohei Ohtani has surprising emotional outburst after bizarre play
item.description: Los Angeles Dodgers star Shohei Ohtani had a surprise outburst of emotion after he was called out on a bizarre play in the fourth inning against the San Diego Padres.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Holy Score: Oddsmaker’s early line has Utah favored over Brigham Young (with or without QB Cam Rising)
item.description: The rivalry game Nov. 9 could have Big 12 title and CFP implications for both teams after BYU's surprising start.
item.dc:creator: Jon Wilner, The Mercury News

item.title: 22-time Grand Slam champion Rafael Nadal will retire from tennis after next month's Davis Cup finals
item.description: MADRID (AP) — Rafael Nadal is retiring from professional tennis at age 38, he announced in a video message Thursday, after winning 22 Grand Slam titles — 14 at the French Open — during an unprecedented era he shared with rivals Roger Federer and Novak Djokovic.
item.dc:creator: TALES AZZONI and HOWARD FENDRICH, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Cal needs ground game to match passing prowess against undefeated No. 22 Pitt
item.description: Three running backs — Jaydn Ott, Jaivian Thomas and Kadarius Calloway — totaled just 23 yards combined on 15 rushes in last week's loss to Miami.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Faraudo, The Mercury News

item.title: Detroit destroys Amon-Ra St. Brown’s brother for comments about city
item.description: The official social media account for the city of Detroit destroyed Amon-Ra St. Brown’s brother for his comments about the city.
item.dc:creator: Derick Hutchinson, Local 4

item.title: The Jacksonville Jaguars say their flight to London will be delayed because of Hurricane Milton
item.description: The Jacksonville Jaguars plan to arrive to London later than expected Friday because of Hurricane Milton, the team confirmed ahead of Sunday’s game against the Chicago Bears.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Sei Young Kim cards 10-under 62 to lead LPGA event in China after first round
item.description: SHANGHAI (AP) — Sei Young Kim, looking for her first victory this season, opened with a 10-under 62 on Thursday to lead the first round of the Buick LPGA Shanghai tournament.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: California lawmakers write letter urging schools to pay athletes directly
item.description: (KRON) -- A pair of California state senators wrote a letter on Wednesday urging schools in the Golden State to change how collegiate athletes are paid.
item.dc:creator: Phil Mayer, KRON4

item.title: Whittingham not ready to hand keys over to Wilson yet
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - With so much uncertainty as to when Cam Rising will be able to return at quarterback for the Utah football team, is there a point when head coach Kyle Whittingham will move on from Rising and hand the keys to the offense over to freshman Isaac Wilson? "There is
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Muggers ripped watch off Dodgers pitcher Walker Buehler's arm, police say
item.description:
Police in California say thieves surrounded Los Angeles Dodgers starting pitcher Walker Buehler last month and ripped a pricey watch off his arm.
Police say the victims were all targets of organized groups who steal high-end watches in large crowds during events.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Tossed balls from stands, apparently aimed at Profar, interrupt Padres' win in NLDS Game 2
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Tempers flared on the field and in the stands at Dodger Stadium, with rowdy fans tossing baseballs, apparently at San Diego left fielder Jurickson Profar, and then trash that caused a 12-minute wait between pitches during the seventh inning of the Padres' 10-2 win in NL Division S
item.dc:creator: BETH HARRIS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Baseball fans discuss Dodgers’ chances in NLDS Game 2 over donuts
item.description: After a rollercoaster of emotions in Game 1 of the National League Divisional Series, baseball lovers were up early getting donuts in Inglewood and eager to share their thoughts with the news on Sunday's matchup between the Los Angeles Dodgers and the San Diego Padres.
item.dc:creator: Will Conybeare, KTLA 5

item.title: Padres fandom in full force at first of three postseason watch parties
item.description: The Padres' quest for postseason glory continues and fans are along for the thrilling journey. Despite Saturday night's game being held in Los Angeles at Dodgers Stadium, the first of three watch parties kicked off with a packed house at Petco Park.
item.dc:creator: Jennifer Franco, Fox 5

item.title: Vanderbilt takes down No. 1 Alabama 40-35 in historic college football victory
item.description: Fans of the Vanderbilt Commodores danced onto the field, jumping up and down as they tore down a goal post, carried it out of the stadium and rang out sweet sounds of victory.
item.dc:creator: TERESA M. WALKER, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Backlash over San Jose State University transgender volleyball player 'isn't about sports,' advocates say
item.description: (KRON) -- Four teams forfeited matches against the San Jose State University women's volleyball team since a lawsuit was filed on behalf of several NCAA athletes, including SJSU's team captain. According to the lawsuit filed on September 23, one of SJSU's volleyball players is transgender.
item.dc:creator: Amy Larson, KRON4

item.title: Indiana Fever star Caitlin Clark a near-unanimous choice as WNBA's Rookie of the Year
item.description: NEW YORK (AP/WGN) — Caitlin Clark has been named the WNBA Rookie of the Year in a near-unanimous vote, giving the Indiana Fever back-to-back winners after Aliyah Boston won the honor last season.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: WFLA's Bucified Bert featured in Amazon Prime show with Bucs' QB Baker Mayfield
item.description: The Tampa Bay Buccaneers are set to face off against the Atlanta Falcons during Thursday Night Football, streaming live tonight on Amazon's Prime Video.
item.dc:creator: Garrett Phillips, WFLA, NBC
